title: status-pipe launch file
description: Committed at .status-pipe/launch.json: how to launch this repo's agent loop. Backend-agnostic process contract. The token %home% in command/args/cwd/env values expands to the user's home directory at spawn time (deliberately not shell-style ${...}), so a committed file need not hard-code an absolute path. A mode:'worker' entry is a TEMPLATE the supervisor instantiates on demand — once per item the planner (mode:'tick') writes to orchestrator.json.dispatch — never scheduled; in a worker entry two extra tokens resolve per dispatched item: %prompt% (the worker's `claude -p` argument) and %worktree% (the worker's cwd). Execution is trust-gated: workspace trust plus per-content-hash approval of the complete entry (command, args, stdin, cwd, env), hashed in its committed token form. Never launched from a linked worktree.

id.description: Unique within the file; supervisor state is keyed (repo, id). When omitted, readers synthesize a positional agent-<index> — set it explicitly if the file lists more than one agent, or reordering re-keys supervisor state.

stdin.description: Written to the child's stdin then closed; supports backends that take their prompt on stdin

env.description: Merged over the inherited environment. Values support %home% (the user's home directory).

mode.description: tick: one pass then exit, relaunched intervalMinutes after exit. daemon: long-running, restarted on death. worker: not scheduled — a template the supervisor instantiates per orchestrator.json.dispatch item, resolving %prompt% (the work-ticket/work-epic argument) and %worktree% (cwd).

intervalMinutes.description: tick mode: minutes of quiet between passes, measured from exit (never overlapping)

timeoutMinutes.description: tick mode: hard wall-clock cap per tick; a pass still running after this is killed and recorded as a failure
